outcome_summary: Successfully negotiated $192K base salary (9.1% increase) plus 2000 additional RSUs and 6-month review guarantee. Counterparty shifted from budget-constrained resistance to creative problem-solving after external leverage was established.

prep_vs_reality.position_accuracy: Moderately accurate - identified counterparty's analytical nature and budget constraints correctly, but underestimated openness to creative compensation structures once external pressure was applied
prep_vs_reality.scenario_accuracy: Good prediction of initial resistance and pivot points, though the specific creative solutions (additional equity + review timeline) weren't fully anticipated in the scenario tree
prep_vs_reality.simulation_accuracy: Strong match on counterparty's measured, analytical communication style and the progression from defensive to collaborative positioning across negotiation rounds

lessons.lesson: External offers carry more psychological weight than just leverage - they reframe the entire conversation from 'can we afford this' to 'how do we retain talent'
lessons.evidence: Sarah's pivot to collaborative problem-solving happened immediately after the FAANG offer was mentioned, shifting from budget defense to creative solutions
lessons.applies_to_future: When negotiating retention with any employer where you have genuine external alternatives

lessons.lesson: Analytical counterparties prefer structured compromise over salary-only solutions
lessons.evidence: Sarah chose to offer additional equity and guaranteed review timeline rather than meet the full salary request, creating a more complex but mutually beneficial package
lessons.applies_to_future: With data-driven managers or in companies with rigid salary bands but flexible equity/benefits structures

lessons.lesson: Budget constraints are often negotiating positions, not hard limits, when retention becomes the priority
lessons.evidence: Initial 'budget constraints' defense disappeared when faced with potential talent loss, revealing room for creative compensation solutions
lessons.applies_to_future: When counterparts lead with budget limitations as their primary objection

pattern_update: Sarah responds to external pressure by becoming more collaborative and creative, preferring structured multi-component solutions over simple salary increases.

scenario_verdicts.notes: Budget constraints were the initial defense but proved flexible when retention became the focus
Exactly as predicted - Sarah shifted to finding creative solutions after round 2
FAANG offer was even more powerful than anticipated, immediately changing the conversation dynamic

simulation_comparison.counterparty_accuracy: Very accurate on communication style and analytical approach, slightly underestimated the speed of pivot to collaborative mode once external leverage was established
